fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 66 is inverted: clamping is applied when `clamp` is False (i.e., `not clamp`) instead of when `clamp` is True, causing extrapolation when clamping is requested and clamping when it is not.

constraints.intent: When clamp is True the branch body (which performs clamping) should be entered, not when clamp is False.

spec.expr: y__4 >= min(y0, y1) and y__4 <= max(y0, y1)

constraints.intent: After the clamping branch executes, the output should be within the anchor demand range.
The bounds should only be computed when clamping is disabled; if clamp is True this code should not execute.

spec.expr: (not clamp) or ((__cbfl_result >= min(y0, y1)) and (__cbfl_result <= max(y0, y1)))

constraints.intent: When clamp is True the returned value must lie within the anchor demand range.
